hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding via steric differentiation.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding is not sufficient — selectivity over SSTR1/3/4/5 is the primary objective. However, the focus must shift to disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin beyond the current best.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must focus on mutating non-pharmacophore positions that are known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved-pocket contacts).
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining the overall peptide scaffold and pharmacophore integrity.
